name: Oncall Review Buddy
tagline: Reads every PR, predicts deploy risk, and pings the right reviewer in Slack with a one-paragraph case for or against the merge.
thesis: Engineers merge risky PRs at 5pm Friday because the reviewer who actually understands the touched code is heads-down on something else. Oncall Review Buddy reads the diff, the relevant CHANGELOG entries, the failing-test history of every file, and the calendar of the eng most familiar with the area — then DMs them a single Slack message: 'this PR touches the billing webhook, last edited by you 11 days ago, has 3 prior P0s tied to similar changes, and is queued to deploy at 17:42 today — green-light or block.' Proof it can be real: deploy-risk scoring is a deterministic blend of commit history, ownership graph, test flakiness, and on-call calendar — no novel ML required.
audience: Eng leads, release captains, and on-call SREs at 50-500 person product orgs shipping multiple times a day.
proofPoints: Risk score combines five deterministic signals — diff size, blast radius, owner availability, recent incidents in touched files, and time-of-day — visible on hover.
Slack DM lands within 90 seconds of PR open, with a one-click 'green-light', 'block', or 'forward' button that updates the PR check.
Pilot at a 220-engineer fintech caught 4 of 5 production-bound regressions over six weeks, with zero false-positive blocks.

launchChecks.label: Ship deterministic risk scorer v1 against GitHub webhooks
Auto-route blocked PRs to the next-most-qualified reviewer
